title: Algolia Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Algolia** is a hosted search-as-a-service platform exposing a REST API across two DNS pools — a DSN-routed read pool (`https://<app_id>-dsn.algolia.net`) and a write pool (`https://<app_id>.algolia.net`). This connector — `AlgoliaConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "algolia"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from an Algolia application:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Indexes | `/1/indexes` | List, create-via-settings, clear, copy, delete |
| Objects | `/1/indexes/{name}/{id}` + `/batch` | Save, get, delete, partial-update single + bulk objects |
| Settings | `/1/indexes/{name}/settings` | Get + put-replace index settings |
| Synonyms | `/1/indexes/{name}/synonyms` | List + save synonyms (1-to-1, expand, alt-correction) |
| Rules | `/1/indexes/{name}/rules` | List + save merchandising rules |
| Browse | `/1/indexes/{name}/browse` | Cursor-paginated full-index export |
| Search | `/1/indexes/{name}/query` | Keyword search with filters + facets |
| Multi-search | `/1/indexes/*/queries` | Federated multi-index search in one round-trip |

The connector normalises Algolia objects fetched via `browse_index` into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), routes all HTTP through `client/http_client.py::AlgoliaHTTPClient` (SOC), and uses the official Algolia host-rotation strategy to survive single-zone outages.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate Algolia API credentials

1. Sign in to https://dashboard.algolia.com.
2. Open **Settings → Team and Access → API Keys** (or click the gear icon → API Keys).
3. Copy your **Application ID** — a short uppercase alphanumeric string (e.g. `LATENCY`).
4. Copy the **Admin API Key** (full read+write) or create a scoped key with the surfaces you need:
   - `listIndexes`, `browse`, `search` — read operations
   - `addObject`, `deleteObject`, `editSettings` — write operations
   - `settings` — index settings management

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Algolia**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `app_id` (text) | Application ID from step 3 |
| `api_key` (secret) | API key from step 4 |
| `default_index` (text, optional) | Default index name (metadata only) |
| `timeout_s` (number, optional) | Per-request timeout, default 30 s |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `app_id` + `api_key` are present, probes `GET /1/indexes` (a quota-free read) to verify the key, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which re-issues `GET /1/indexes` as a lightweight liveness probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED`. On transport-level failure across every host in the rotation → `OFFLINE + CONNECTED` with a structured-log warning.

### 4. First search

```python
result = await connector.search_index(
    index_name="products",
    query="shoes",
    filters="category:running AND price < 200",
    hits_per_page=20,
)
print(result["hits"])
```

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Algolia REST API uses **server-to-server API key authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `app_id` is sent in the `X-Algolia-Application-Id` header on every request.
- `api_key` is sent in the `X-Algolia-API-Key` header on every request.
- Neither credential is ever sent in a query string — Algolia explicitly recommends headers for server-to-server traffic.

### DSN routing (Algolia-specific gotcha)

Algolia runs two distinct DNS pools and the connector routes reads vs writes accordingly:

| Pool | Host pattern | Use |
|---|---|---|
| **Read** | `<app_id>-dsn.algolia.net` | Geo-DNS routes the caller to the lowest-latency PoP. Used for `is_alive`, `list_indexes`, `get_index_settings`, `browse_index`, `search_index`, `multi_search`, `get_object`, `list_synonyms`, `list_rules`. |
| **Write** | `<app_id>.algolia.net` | Single write primary. Used for `create_index_settings`, `save_object`, `save_objects`, `delete_object`, `partial_update_object`, `clear_index`, `delete_index`, `save_synonym`, `save_rule`, `copy_index`. |
| **Fallback** | `<app_id>-{1,2,3}.algolianet.com` | Shuffled, used when the primary read or write host returns 5xx or transport error. Separate DNS zone so a `algolia.net` outage does not bring down the connector. |

`build_read_hosts` / `build_write_hosts` in `helpers/utils.py` are the single owner of host rotation.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `app_id` + `api_key`. Probes `GET /1/indexes`. Persists config. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo(access_token=api_key, token_type="api_key")` for surface compatibility — no exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /1/indexes` — same probe as install. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
X-Algolia-Application-Id: <app_id>
X-Algolia-API-Key:        <api_key>
Content-Type:             application/json
Accept:                   application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `app_id` | text | yes | `self.app_id` | `X-Algolia-Application-Id` header |
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | `X-Algolia-API-Key` header |
| `default_index` | text | no | `self.default_index` | Metadata only — surfaced to the dashboard |
| `timeout_s` | number | no | `self.timeout_s` (default 30) | Per-request httpx timeout |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["app_id", "api_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED",  "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
```

### Retry constants (`helpers/utils.py`)

```python
RETRY_DELAY_S    = 1.0     # base delay for with_retry()
BACKOFF_FACTOR   = 2.0     # exponential multiplier
MAX_RETRY_DELAY_S = 32.0   # ceiling per attempt
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `AlgoliaConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::AlgoliaHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `app_id` + `api_key`; probe `GET /1/indexes`; persist config. Maps every failure to a typed status — never raises.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No OAuth exchange for api_key. Returns `TokenInfo(access_token=api_key, token_type="api_key")`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /1/indexes` as a lightweight liveness check. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Default (`full=False`) — enumerates indexes via `list_indexes`, normalises each into a `NormalizedDocument(source="algolia.index")`, and ingests. With `full=True` — additionally browses every index via `browse_index` (cursor pagination) and ingests each object as `NormalizedDocument(source=f"algolia.{index_name}")`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Indexes
children.content:
### `async list_indexes() -> Dict`
`GET /1/indexes` — list all indexes. Returns `{items: [{name, entries, dataSize, fileSize, lastBuildTimeS, primary?}], nbPages}`.

### `async create_index_settings(index_name, settings) -> Dict`
`PUT /1/indexes/{name}/settings` — replace index settings. Algolia creates the index lazily on the first push. Returns `{taskID, updatedAt}`.

### `async get_index_settings(index_name) -> Dict`
`GET /1/indexes/{name}/settings` — fetch the full settings object (searchable attributes, ranking, faceting, custom ranking, etc.).

children.title: Objects
children.content:
### `async save_object(index_name, object_data, object_id=None) -> Dict`
`PUT /1/indexes/{name}/{id}` (with id) or `POST /1/indexes/{name}` (Algolia generates id). Returns `{taskID, objectID, createdAt|updatedAt}`.

### `async save_objects(index_name, objects, action="addObject") -> Dict`
`POST /1/indexes/{name}/batch` — bulk indexing. Wire body: `{requests: [{action, body}, …]}`. Valid actions: `addObject` | `updateObject` | `partialUpdateObject` | `partialUpdateObjectNoCreate` | `deleteObject`.

### `async get_object(index_name, object_id, attributes=None) -> Dict`
`GET /1/indexes/{name}/{id}` — fetch a single object. Optional `attributes=[...]` projects to specific fields via `?attributesToRetrieve=`.

### `async delete_object(index_name, object_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /1/indexes/{name}/{id}` — delete one object. Returns `{taskID, deletedAt}`.

### `async partial_update_object(index_name, object_id, attributes, create_if_not_exists=True) -> Dict`
`POST /1/indexes/{name}/{id}/partial?createIfNotExists=<bool>` — patch specific attributes (preserve unspecified). Set `create_if_not_exists=False` for strict-update semantics.

children.title: Browse · Search
children.content:
### `async browse_index(index_name, *, cursor=None, params=None) -> Dict`
`POST /1/indexes/{name}/browse` — cursor-paginated full-index export (bypasses the search engine, returns raw records). First call: omit `cursor`. Subsequent calls: pass the `cursor` from the previous response. Iteration ends when the response omits `cursor`.

### `async search_index(index_name, query, *, filters=None, hits_per_page=20, page=0, extra=None) -> Dict`
`POST /1/indexes/{name}/query` — single-index keyword search with filters + pagination. `extra` merges additional Algolia search parameters (facets, attributesToRetrieve, restrictSearchableAttributes, etc.).

### `async multi_search(requests) -> Dict`
`POST /1/indexes/*/queries` — federated multi-index search in one round-trip. Each request: `{indexName, query, …}`. Returns `{results: [{hits, nbHits, …}, …]}`.

children.title: Synonyms · Rules
children.content:
### `async list_synonyms(index_name, *, query="", type=None, page=0, hits_per_page=100) -> Dict`
`POST /1/indexes/{name}/synonyms/search` — paginated synonym search. Optional `type` filters by `synonym | onewaysynonym | altcorrection1 | altcorrection2 | placeholder`.

### `async save_synonym(index_name, synonym_id, synonym, forward_to_replicas=False) -> Dict`
`PUT /1/indexes/{name}/synonyms/{id}?forwardToReplicas=<bool>` — upsert a synonym record. Body shape depends on `type`:
```json
{"objectID":"syn-1", "type":"synonym", "synonyms":["car","auto","automobile"]}
{"objectID":"syn-2", "type":"onewaysynonym", "input":"car", "synonyms":["auto"]}
```

### `async list_rules(index_name, *, query="", page=0, hits_per_page=100) -> Dict`
`POST /1/indexes/{name}/rules/search` — paginated search for merchandising rules.

### `async save_rule(index_name, rule_id, rule, forward_to_replicas=False) -> Dict`
`PUT /1/indexes/{name}/rules/{id}?forwardToReplicas=<bool>` — upsert a merchandising rule. Rule shape:
```json
{
    "objectID": "rule-1",
    "conditions": [{"pattern": "shoes", "anchoring": "contains"}],
    "consequence": {
        "params": {"filters": "category:shoes"},
        "promote": [{"objectID": "prod-1", "position": 0}]
    },
    "enabled": true
}
```

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
AlgoliaError                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── AlgoliaAuthError                  # 401 / 403
├── AlgoliaBadRequestError            # 400
├── AlgoliaNotFound                   # 404
├── AlgoliaRateLimitError             # 429 — retry_after_s
├── AlgoliaServerError                # 5xx — host-rotation candidate
└── AlgoliaNetworkError               # all hosts exhausted
```

Back-compat aliases:
```
AlgoliaNotFoundError = AlgoliaNotFound
```

### Host rotation (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

Algolia's official recovery strategy for 5xx is **a different host**, not a longer wait — so the client iterates the rotation and only raises after every host fails.

| Condition | Action |
|---|---|
| 2xx | Parse body, return |
| 400 | Raise `AlgoliaBadRequestError` immediately (same on every node) |
| 401 / 403 | Raise `AlgoliaAuthError` immediately |
| 404 | Raise `AlgoliaNotFound` immediately |
| 429 | Raise `AlgoliaRateLimitError` immediately |
| 5xx | Capture, try next host |
| Transport error (DNS / connect / timeout) | Capture, try next host |
| All hosts exhausted | Raise `AlgoliaNetworkError` |

### Higher-level retry (`helpers/utils.py::with_retry`)

Wraps every public method in `connector.py`. Retries `AlgoliaRateLimitError` and `AlgoliaNetworkError` with exponential backoff (`1 s × 2^attempt + jitter`, capped at 32 s, default 3 attempts). All other exceptions propagate unchanged so callers can branch on type.

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED) — logged as warning
5xx → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   CONNECTED) — host rotation already tried
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `app_id` or `api_key` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Application ID is uppercase alphanumeric — verify exact match. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at install / health | 401 from Algolia — key revoked, wrong app_id, or key rotated | In Algolia Dashboard → API Keys, verify the key still exists. Rotate if necessary and re-enter in ACP. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health | 403 — API key scope too narrow (e.g. search-only key trying a write endpoint) | Upgrade to the Admin API Key OR add the missing ACL (`addObject`, `editSettings`, …) to the scoped key. |
| `AlgoliaRateLimitError` raised under load | Plan quota exhausted (operations or search) | `with_retry` already backs off; reduce concurrency, increase batch size for writes (one `save_objects` call costs 1 op regardless of batch size), or upgrade plan. |
| `AlgoliaNetworkError: all hosts failed` | Every host in the rotation returned 5xx or transport error | Check https://status.algolia.com. If only one region is affected, fallback hosts on `algolianet.com` should already have caught it — verify outbound DNS allows `algolianet.com` from your egress. |
| 404 on `get_index_settings(name)` | Index does not exist yet | Call `create_index_settings(name, settings)` first — Algolia creates the index lazily on the first settings PUT. |
| `browse_index` never terminates | Loop forgot to honor missing `cursor` in response | The loop should exit when the response body omits the `cursor` key. `browse_index` returns `{hits, cursor?}` — absence of `cursor` means end of iteration. |
| `save_objects` batch returns fewer objectIDs than sent | Some records were rejected (validation, schema) | Inspect `taskID` via `get_task` (if available) and check Algolia Dashboard → Logs for per-record errors. Use `partial_update_object` for incremental fixes. |
| Replica indexes diverge from primary | `forward_to_replicas=False` on `save_synonym` / `save_rule` | Set `forward_to_replicas=True` to propagate changes; or call the API per-replica explicitly. |
| DSN host slow but writes are fast | Caller's region is far from the nearest Algolia PoP | DSN geo-DNS already picks the lowest-latency PoP — slowness usually indicates network egress (corporate proxy, VPN). The fallback ring is on a different DNS zone but the same regions. |
